assumptions: Mechanism completeness is provisional unless the chemical domain and operating range are named.
Thermodynamic and kinetic data should not be extrapolated silently.
interpretation_rules: Separate chemical validity from numerical convergence.
Flag mechanism or source-range gaps as blocking when they control the answer.
label: Chemistry & Chemical Modeling Basis
principles: Chemical models require explicit species, reactions, thermodynamic basis, and validity ranges.
Stoichiometry and conservation checks precede kinetic or thermochemical conclusions.
Source ranges and units are governing context for chemical predictions.
reference_points: Known property or rate reference values.
summary: Chemical-model basis for species, stoichiometry, thermochemistry, kinetics, mechanisms, and validity ranges.

assumptions: Numerical tolerances are problem-specific and must be recorded.
Local runtime behavior is not trusted without a deterministic or benchmark-backed check.
interpretation_rules: Treat numerical output as evidence only when solver assumptions and verification checks are visible.
Escalate rather than silently accepting unstable or unconverged results.
label: Computational Engineering Numerical Methods
principles: Algorithm choice follows equation class, scale, stiffness, constraints, and required confidence.
Convergence, conservation, and sensitivity checks are part of the result, not optional commentary.
Execution context must be bounded and reproducible for governed engineering runs.
reference_points: Known benchmark or simplified reference cases.
summary: Numerical-method basis for solver selection, discretization, convergence, stability, sensitivity, and reproducible execution.

assumptions: Default process capability should be treated as provisional unless a specific machine/process is named.
Inspection strategy is part of acceptance when tolerances are function-critical.
interpretation_rules: Do not infer manufacturability from CAD validity alone.
Surface hidden tolerance or inspection assumptions as open issues.
label: Manufacturing Process Basis
principles: Geometry is not manufacturable until process constraints, tolerance stackups, and inspection methods are considered.
Design intent must survive translation from CAD to manufacturing and simulation artifacts.
Process capability sets practical limits on achievable geometry and tolerances.
reference_points: Units and datum consistency between CAD, mesh, and manufacturing outputs.
Process-feasibility check against named manufacturing route.
summary: Manufacturing basis for process capability, feature feasibility, tolerancing, inspection, and geometry handoff.

assumptions: Material models and boundary conditions are approximate until validated against evidence or standard references.
Stress concentrations and contact behavior require special interpretation before design acceptance.
interpretation_rules: Do not treat a solver output as a mechanical conclusion until load paths and constraints have been checked.
Prefer conservative interpretation when assumptions are under-specified.
label: Mechanical Engineering Mechanics Basis
principles: Mechanical conclusions depend on explicitly stated loads, constraints, material properties, and failure criteria.
Tolerance and assembly assumptions should be represented as first-class constraints when they affect function.
Closed-form or simplified static references are preferred sanity anchors before trusting high-fidelity simulation.
reference_points: Free-body force balance for static loads.
Dimensional consistency for stress, strain, stiffness, and torque calculations.
Mesh or discretization convergence for simulation-backed claims.
summary: Mechanics basis for load paths, material behavior, constraints, stresses, strains, tolerances, and mechanical validation.

assumptions: Standards may be stale or jurisdiction-specific until their revision and applicability are confirmed.
Reference tables require declared interpolation/extrapolation policy.
interpretation_rules: Do not convert a reference into a requirement unless the mode/policy says it is governing.
Record conflicts between standards and project constraints as explicit open issues.
label: Standards & Data Interpretation Basis
principles: A standard or table governs only inside its stated scope, revision, and applicability limits.
Units, source provenance, and stale-reference risk must be explicit in governed artifacts.
Static references anchor verification but do not replace project-specific acceptance criteria.
reference_points: Static benchmark or table row used as acceptance evidence.
summary: Source-bearing basis for standards, static data, governing references, units, benchmark interpretation, and applicability limits.
